concerns.text: FR-011 requires sensitivity analysis for 15-30% time reduction threshold with false-positive/false-negative rate sweeps. The idea only states this as an expected result, not a required analysis. This is over-reach beyond the stated research question.

Constitution VII (Human Participant Ethics) requires informed consent collection before data collection. The spec's US-1 acceptance scenarios do not include consent capture as a prerequisite for experiment execution. This is under-reach on an explicit constitutional requirement.
The idea specifies '100 medium-difficulty Codeforces problems' but the spec only mentions 'Codeforces problems' without quantity. This should be clarified for reproducibility (Constitution I).

The idea specifies JaCoText (Java) and StarCoder (Python) as LLM assistants. The spec's assumptions mention these models but the FRs do not explicitly require their use. Consider adding FR for model selection to ensure idea fidelity.
